mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a win rate above 52 percent, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through momentum and earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5 percent annualized growth rate, inflation expectations detach from central bank targets, forcing markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening and reduced real corporate margins, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.5 percent correlates with positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations, or the signal fails to predict direction when real yields are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a win rate above 50 percent, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter expansion in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic growth and validates corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through fundamental valuation channels.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle phases, or the predictive relationship reverses when GDPC1 growth exceeds 4 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and consumer prices indicate supply-constrained demand pulling through the economy, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate above 55 percent, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
